name: Financial Services
description: Banking, insurance, and capital markets AI regulation including credit scoring, fraud detection, algorithmic trading, and automated underwriting.

highRiskCategories.description: AI systems used to evaluate creditworthiness or establish credit scores of natural persons, with the exception of AI systems used for detecting financial fraud.

highRiskCategories.category: Risk assessment and pricing in life and health insurance
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems used for risk assessment and pricing in relation to natural persons in the case of life and health insurance.

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement model risk management frameworks for AI/ML models used in credit decisions, ensuring independent model validation, ongoing monitoring, and documented model inventories.
sectorRequirements.source: MAS FEAT Principles; BNM Risk Management in Technology (RMiT); SR 11-7 (US Fed)

sectorRequirements.requirement: Ensure AI-driven credit scoring systems provide meaningful explanations of adverse decisions to affected individuals, including the principal factors that led to the decision.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 86; Equal Credit Opportunity Act (US); MAS FEAT Fairness Principle

sectorRequirements.requirement: Conduct fairness testing and bias impact assessments for lending, insurance underwriting, and customer segmentation models to prevent discrimination on protected characteristics.
sectorRequirements.source: MAS FEAT Fairness Principle; EU AI Act Art. 10; FCA Consumer Duty; BNM Fair Treatment of Financial Consumers

sectorRequirements.requirement: Maintain comprehensive data lineage and quality controls for training data used in financial AI models, with particular attention to representativeness and historical bias in financial datasets.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 10; MAS FEAT Ethics Principle; ISO 42001 Annex A

sectorRequirements.requirement: Establish human-in-the-loop requirements for high-value automated decisions including loan approvals above thresholds, insurance claim denials, and trading halts.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 14; MAS Guidelines on Individual Accountability; Singapore MAIGF Human-in-the-Loop

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement AI-specific incident response procedures for model failures, adversarial attacks, data poisoning, and unexpected model drift in production financial systems.
sectorRequirements.source: MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines; BNM RMiT; NIST AI RMF MANAGE function

sectorRequirements.requirement: Register high-risk AI systems used in credit scoring and insurance pricing in the EU AI database, and maintain conformity assessment documentation.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 49, Art. 43; OECD AI Principles Transparency

sectorRequirements.requirement: Conduct regular algorithmic audits of trading algorithms, robo-advisory systems, and market surveillance AI to ensure market integrity and consumer protection.
sectorRequirements.source: MiFID II Art. 17 (EU); MAS Securities and Futures Act; FCA Algorithmic Trading Compliance

keyRisks.description: AI credit models may perpetuate or amplify historical lending discrimination against protected groups through biased training data or proxy variables.
Complex ML models used for insurance pricing may be unable to provide legally required explanations for premium calculations or claim denials.
AI-driven algorithmic trading systems may contribute to market instability through correlated automated responses to market signals.
Adversarial actors may manipulate transaction patterns to train fraud detection models to miss specific attack vectors.
Widespread adoption of similar AI risk models across the sector may create correlated failures and systemic risk during stress events.
